zh.Halstead St Andrew, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭艾可哈爾斯亞德 聖 安德魯
zh.Sible Hedingham, Essex, England: 英國艾可希布爾 丁加姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Bourchier, 1st Baron Bourchier於1306年之後出生在英國艾可哈爾斯亞德時，他的父親John Bourchier8710歲，母親Helen Colchester8719歲。他於大約1338年與Margaret Prayers在英國艾可希布爾 丁加姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1335年登記服兵役。他於1334年被列為appointed chief justice of the king's bench in ireland (but never took up office)。他去世於大約1348年8月20日在法國延波迪奧，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可哈爾斯亞德 聖 安德魯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Bourchier, 1st Baron Bourchier于1306年之后出生在英国艾可哈爾斯亞德时，他的父亲John Bourchier8710岁，母亲Helen Colchester8719岁。他于大约1338年与Margaret Prayers在英国艾可希布爾 丁加姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1335年登记服兵役。他于1334年被列为appointed chief justice of the king's bench in ireland (but never took up office)。他去世于大约1348年8月20日在法国延波迪奧，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可哈爾斯亞德 聖 安德魯。

nameMeanings.name: Bourchier,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German   Buser  .
English (Kent): variant of   Bowser   1.

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: North German (also Börger) and Dutch: variant of Burger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from Yiddish borger ‘borrower’ or ‘lender’.
Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name for someone from a place called Borg (see   Borg  ), or a topographic name for someone living by a fortification or near a hill resembling a fortification.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Bourchier, 1st Baron Bourchier was born after 1306, in Halstead, Essex, England, his father, John Bourchier, was 8710 and his mother, Helen Colchester, was 8719. He married Margaret Prayers about 1338, in Sible Hedingham, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He registered for military service in 1335. In 1334, his occupation is listed as appointed chief justice of the king's bench in ireland (but never took up office). He died about 20 August 1348, in Bordeaux, Guyenne, France, and was buried in Halstead St Andrew, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Margaret Prayers environ 1338, à Sible Hedingham, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1335. En 1334, sa profession de son indiquée est appointed chief justice of the king's bench in ireland (but never took up office). Il est décédé environ 20 août 1348, à Bordeaux, Guyenne, France, et a été enterré à Halstead St Andrew, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.Halstead St Andrew, Essex, England: 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 할스테아드 스트 앤드루
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Bourchier, 1st Baron Bourchier(이)가 1306년 이후 영국 에세스 할스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Bourchier(은)는 8710세였고, 그의 어머니 Helen Colchester(은)는 8719세였다. 그는 대략 1338년 영국 에세스 시블일 헤딩함에서 Margaret Prayers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1335년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1334년, 그의 직업은 appointed chief justice of the king's bench in ireland (but never took up office)이었다. 그는 대략 1348년 8월 20일 프랑스 가예니 볼도스에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 할스테아드 스트 앤드루에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Margaret Prayers aproximadamente 1338, em Sible Hedingham, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1335. Em 1334, sua profissão aparece como appointed chief justice of the king's bench in ireland (but never took up office). Ele faleceu aproximadamente 20 de agosto de 1348, em Bordeaux, Guyenne, França, e foi sepultado em Halstead St Andrew, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Halstead St Andrew, Essex, England: холстид ст андрю, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Bourchier, 1st Baron Bourchier родился после 1306 г. в местности холстид, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени John Bourchier было 8710 лет, а матери, которую звали Helen Colchester, 8719. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Prayers около 1338 г. в местности сибл хедингхам, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. В 1335 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1334 годуработал по профессии appointed chief justice of the king's bench in ireland (but never took up office). Он умер около 20 августа 1348 г. в местности бордокс, гуиенн, Франция и был похоронен в местности холстид ст андрю, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
